text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence that can understand and generate human-like text. They work by learning patterns in language from massive amounts of written material, known as training data. This data can include books, articles, and websites. After training, users interact with the model using prompts, which are instructions or questions. Prompt engineering is the skill of writing effective prompts to get better results. LLMs have impressive capabilities, such as writing essays, answering questions, or translating languages. However, they have limits. They do not truly understand meaning and can produce incorrect or biased information, so it is important to use their output with caution.
